evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: Initiate Transaction endpoint /theia/api/v1/initiateTransaction, Transaction Status /v3/order/status, CheckoutJS init/invoke pattern, txnToken/TXN_TOKEN all match Paytm conventions. No version explicitly claimed but method names are self-consistent across backend and frontend code blocks.
evidence.executability: PaytmChecksum is imported via 'from PaytmChecksum import PaytmChecksum' with no pip install command shown anywhere — extractor has no install line for it. No requirements list at all. Also requires ngrok for callback testing (mentioned in prose, not as command). React component uses process.env.REACT_APP_* but no .env file extractable for frontend was scoped clearly. Multiple non-trivial fixes needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) Acquisition guidance vague ('Paytm dashboard, developer settings') — no explicit URL. (b) NO pip install or npm install bash blocks anywhere — major gap. (c) env template present for both backend .env and frontend .env. (d) backend code present. (e) frontend code present. (f) No test command in a bash block — only prose about testing. Missing install commands and test command.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bare bracketed numbers like [2][10][30] with no URLs, no domain references. No canonical Paytm docs URLs (developer.paytm.com or business.paytm.com/docs) appear anywhere as actual links. Effectively no anchored citations.

rubric_notes: Very long, prose-heavy. No bash install blocks despite multiple code files. ngrok mentioned in prose but not as executable command — still flagged as sandbox concern. docs_unavailable=true so freshness scored on internal consistency (good).
